title: Bridge Essay: The restorative effects of short-term exposure to nature in immersive virtual environments (IVEs) as evidenced by participants' brain activities.
concept: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

**The restorative effects of short-term exposure to nature in immersive virtual environments (IVEs) as evidenced by participants' brain activities.** - J Environ Manage (2023). DOI: 10.1016/j.jenvman.2022.116830. PMID: 36435136. Short-term exposure to nature has excellent potential to be used as a public health intervention measure. Nevertheless, the physiological and psychological mechanisms of this health benefit are still unclear. In this study, we intend to verify the effects of short-term exposure to nature on psychological functioning and to explore the underlying mechanism through experiments conducted in immersive virtual environments (IVEs).
